title: Fix Swagger UI auth: /token must return OAuth2-compliant top-level response
description: Swagger UI Authorize sukses, tapi request endpoint terproteksi mengembalikan 'Authentication required'. Penyebab: /token membungkus access_token di dalam field 'data' (via ResponseBuilder.created), sehingga Swagger UI menyimpan token=undefined dan mengirim 'Authorization: Bearer undefined'. Solusi: /token mengembalikan Token model langsung (top-level access_token/token_type/expires_in/refresh_token) sesuai OAuth2PasswordRequestForm spec. /refresh disamakan agar konsisten.
close_reason: Returned Token/BaseToken directly from /token and /refresh so Swagger UI's OAuth2 flow can parse top-level access_token. App import smoke-tested OK.

title: Investigate & fix 401 Unauthorized regression at /token login post-refactor
description: Setelah merge commit 1253876 (refactor main.py: CORS helper + JWT handler collapse + ResponseBuilder normalization), login via Swagger UI gagal dengan status 401 Unauthorized. Sebelum refactor login berjalan normal. Perlu investigasi reproduksi, isolasi seam mana yang regress (CORS preflight, OAuth2 form parsing, atau response-builder mapping), dan perbaiki tanpa mengembalikan tiga keluhan yang sudah diselesaikan oleh issue ly5/5b9/j0u.
design:
Hipotesis kandidat akar masalah (diurut dari paling mungkin):

1. CORS preflight regression — Swagger UI di browser tertentu menganggap POST /token sebagai cross-origin. cors_allow_methods & cors_allow_headers di Config dimuat sebagai string ('*') bukan list; cors_allow_credentials default False. Jika preflight gagal, Swagger menampilkan error generik yang user persepsikan sebagai 'not authorized'.

2. OAuth2PasswordRequestForm parsing — refactor tidak menyentuh /token route, tapi perubahan middleware bisa memengaruhi parsing form. Periksa apakah Content-Type application/x-www-form-urlencoded masih lolos.

3. Response shape berubah — handler PyJWTError baru memanggil ResponseBuilder.unauthorized(message, headers=...). Parameter pertama unauthorized bernama 'errors', bukan 'message'. Untuk login (/token) ini tidak relevan karena /token tidak melempar PyJWTError, tapi /me akan menampilkan field salah posisi.

4. ResponseBuilder static-vs-instance breakage — auth.py memanggil response_builder.from_http_exception(e) via instance dari Depends. Setelah promosi ke classmethod, panggilan via instance tetap valid di Python. Verifikasi tidak ada regresi.

Yang BUKAN penyebab (sudah dieliminasi):
- /token tidak menggunakan oauth2_scheme; murni form-data + MySQL PASSWORD() check.
- TokenIssuer & TokenVerifier tidak disentuh refactor ini.
- SysUserRepository tidak disentuh.

title: Tambah endpoint GET /tunkin/exists/{periode}
description: Tambahkan endpoint baru pada app/tunkin/router.py dengan path '/exists/{periode}' untuk mengecek apakah sudah ada data tunkin di periode tertentu. Response berupa status exist (boolean) dan jumlah data (count) di periode tersebut. Endpoint harus mengikuti pola existing: gunakan require_role(['payrollprocess']), ResponseBuilder, dan TunkinRepository (tambahkan method baru misal count_by_periode di repository).
acceptance_criteria: 1. Endpoint GET /tunkin/exists/{periode} tersedia. 2. Response berisi field 'exists' (bool) dan 'count' (int). 3. Menggunakan require_role(['payrollprocess']). 4. Repository memiliki method untuk hitung data per periode.

title: Validate file periode against request periode in upload flow
description: Pada logic pemrosesan Excel di TunkinRepository.upload (atau setara), setelah pandas membaca file, bandingkan setiap nilai kolom periode di file dengan periode dari request. Jika ada selisih (atau kolom periode kosong), throw HTTPException dengan status 400 dan pesan yang menjelaskan baris/nilai yang tidak sesuai. Jangan lakukan insert ke database ketika validasi gagal.
acceptance_criteria: 1. Bila semua baris file memiliki periode == request.periode, upload sukses seperti biasa. 2. Bila ada baris dengan periode berbeda atau kosong, endpoint mengembalikan 400 dengan pesan jelas dan tidak menyentuh database.

title: Wire TunkinUploadRequest into /upload endpoint signature
description: Pada app/tunkin/router.py, ganti parameter file: UploadFile pada endpoint POST /tunkin/upload dengan TunkinUploadRequest dari app/models/request_model.py sehingga endpoint menerima field periode (form/body) dan file (UploadFile) sekaligus. Pastikan FastAPI dapat memparsing request multipart/form-data dengan benar (gunakan Depends/Form jika perlu karena model mengandung UploadFile).
acceptance_criteria: Endpoint /upload menerima TunkinUploadRequest, dan request body multipart berisi periode + file dapat di-parse tanpa error oleh FastAPI.

title: Pass periode from request to UploadKpiCommand.execute
description: UploadKpiCommand.execute saat ini hanya menerima file. Update signature command (app/tunkin/commands.py) dan caller di router agar periode dari TunkinUploadRequest diteruskan ke command, lalu ke repository upload, sehingga validasi periode dapat dilakukan di layer pemrosesan file.
acceptance_criteria: UploadKpiCommand.execute menerima periode + file, dan nilai periode dari request body terpropagasi sampai ke logic upload di TunkinRepository.

title: Refactor /upload endpoint to use TunkinUploadRequest with periode validation
description: Ubah endpoint POST /tunkin/upload pada app/tunkin/router.py untuk menggunakan model TunkinUploadRequest dari app/models/request_model.py (yang berisi field periode dan file). Selain itu, tambahkan validasi: saat memproses file Excel, jika nilai kolom periode di dalam file tidak sesuai dengan periode pada request body, harus throw error (misal HTTPException 400) sebelum data disimpan.
acceptance_criteria: 1. Endpoint /upload menerima TunkinUploadRequest (periode + file) sebagai input. 2. Saat upload, sistem membandingkan periode di request dengan periode di tiap baris file Excel. 3. Jika ada baris dengan periode != request.periode, endpoint mengembalikan error yang jelas dan tidak menyimpan data. 4. Endpoint tetap mengembalikan response sukses via ResponseBuilder ketika semua periode valid.

title: Remove try/except from routers, rely on @app.exception_handler in main.py
description: main.py already registers HTTPException + Exception handlers. Per-route try/except in tunkin.py and auth.py duplicates the work and even has a silent fall-through bug in tunkin.upload_file (status codes other than 400/500 return None). Delete try/except, let exceptions propagate.

title: Move tunkin into app/tunkin/ domain module + introduce UploadKpiCommand
description: Consolidate to app/tunkin/{router,commands,repository,services,schemas}.py. Introduce UploadKpiCommand(file_gate, parser, kpi_repo).execute(file) -> UpsertResult so the upload pipeline is reachable from non-HTTP entrypoints (CLI, future workers). GET /{periode} stays repo-direct (no handler — list-style endpoint, see CQRS rule). TunkinRepository keeps its JOIN to organization (ADR-0001).

title: Move auth code into app/auth/ domain module
description: Consolidate auth pieces into one domain folder: app/routers/auth.py -> app/auth/router.py; app/repositories/sys_user.py + sys_menu.py -> app/auth/repository.py; existing app/auth/permission_checker.py + menu_lookup.py -> app/auth/permissions.py. KEEP app/core/security.py where it is (require_role, TokenIssuer, TokenVerifier are cross-cutting deps used by every domain — see grilling decision).

title: Split response_model.py (434 lines) into responses/ package
description: response_model.py is 434 lines mixing pydantic schemas (BaseResponse, Token, User) with HTTP serialization (ResponseBuilder + error dispatch registry). Split into app/responses/schemas.py, app/responses/builder.py, app/responses/errors.py per architecture review. See CONTEXT.md and grilling session 2026-05-13.

title: Create app/organization/ domain module with GET /organization endpoint
description: New public read-only endpoint feeding the FE Tunkin-page filter. Domain layout: router.py (GET /organization, no auth), repository.py (OrganizationRepository.list), schemas.py (Organization, OrganizationFilter), inline get_X() factories per file. Mounted on app in main.py. Has its own SELECT — does NOT replace the JOIN in TunkinRepository (see ADR-0001).

title: Collapse duplicate JWT exception handlers in app/main.py
description:
app/main.py defines three exception handlers all named expired_signature_exception_handler (lines 27-45), one for ExpiredSignatureError, one for InvalidTokenError, one for DecodeError. The function name collision is misleading, the request parameter is unused (IDE warning), and each handler is a shallow copy-paste that just calls response_builder.unauthorized().

Goal: collapse the three handlers into a single, well-named handler that covers all JWT errors, removing the duplication and silencing the IDE warning about the unused request argument.

Out of scope: changing how routers raise these errors, or altering the response shape.
design:
Treat JWT-decoding failures as one error class:
- PyJWT's ExpiredSignatureError, InvalidTokenError, and DecodeError all inherit from PyJWTError; one handler can cover all three.
- The handler doesn't need request, so rename it to _request (or _) to signal 'intentionally unused' and silence the linter.
- Keep the WWW-Authenticate: Bearer header on the response, which is what triggers the browser/Swagger to re-authenticate.
- The handler should still distinguish 'expired' vs 'invalid' in the user-facing message — use isinstance to branch on the message, but keep the response builder call in one place.
acceptance_criteria:
1. Only one handler function for JWT errors, with a unique, descriptive name.
2. No IDE warning about unused request parameter.
3. Behavior unchanged: expired tokens return 401 with 'expired'-style message; invalid/undecodable tokens return 401 with 'invalid'-style message; all responses include WWW-Authenticate: Bearer.
4. Existing /token, /refresh, /me, /validate endpoints still behave the same when fed bad tokens.

title: Refactor CORS middleware setup in app/main.py
description:
The CORS middleware in app/main.py:14-20 is wired inline with allow_origins=['*'] and missing kwargs, which triggers an IDE warning and leaves the CORS policy as a shallow, hard-coded seam.

Goal: turn the CORS configuration into a deeper module driven by configuration, so the policy can be changed without editing main.py and so the IDE warning disappears.

Out of scope: changing other middleware, touching routers, or altering authentication behavior.
design:
Treat CORS as a configurable seam:
- Source allowed origins, methods, headers, and credentials flag from Config (env vars), not literals.
- Wire CORS through a single helper that takes the FastAPI app and the Config, so main.py only calls one line.
- Default to permissive in development, restrictive in production via env.
- Make sure all CORSMiddleware kwargs are passed explicitly so the IDE stops complaining.
- Remember that allow_origins=['*'] is incompatible with allow_credentials=True per the CORS spec — pick one.
acceptance_criteria:
1. main.py no longer has IDE warning on CORSMiddleware.
2. CORS origins/methods/headers are read from environment variables via Config.
3. main.py contains a single helper call for CORS setup (no inline kwargs soup).
4. Behavior unchanged for the default dev configuration.

title: Split Excel upload monolith into FileGate, KPISheetParser, and KPIRepository.upsert_batch
description:
TunkinRepository.upload() (app/repositories/tunkin_repository.py:66–172) orchestrates four concerns in one method: file validation (extension/size/MIME), Excel parsing via pandas, row-shape validation, and bulk upsert. The interface is shallow — callers pass an UploadFile and User, and the same complexity sits behind it rearranged. Nothing is unit-testable without a real Excel file and DB.

Split into three modules behind narrow interfaces:
- FileGate.check(UploadFile) → validated bytes (or raise)
- KPISheetParser.parse(bytes, column_spec) → list[KPIRecord]
- KPIRepository.upsert_batch(records) → result

The router composes them. End-to-end: POST /tunkin/upload still works for a valid file and still returns the existing validation errors for malformed inputs.
acceptance_criteria: FileGate, KPISheetParser, and KPIRepository are separate modules each with unit tests that touch neither FastAPI nor MySQL; POST /tunkin/upload integration test passes for a golden-path Excel file; existing validation errors (bad extension, bad MIME, oversize, missing columns) return identical HTTP responses to before; no method in the new modules exceeds ~40 LOC

title: Move Sqids ID encoding out of DatabaseHelper.fetch_page to HTTP edge; make encoding deterministic
description:
DatabaseHelper.fetch_page (app/core/databases.py:108) directly encodes IDs via SqidsHelper — a presentation concern leaking into the data layer. Additionally, SqidsHelper.encode() (app/core/config.py:44–50) mixes datetime.now() parts into the payload, so the same ID encodes differently across calls; round-tripping is broken and tests cannot assert on encoded IDs.

fetch_page should return raw integer IDs. ID encoding moves to the HTTP edge — either inside ResponseBuilder.page() or a small IDCodec applied during page serialization. SqidsHelper.encode([id]) becomes deterministic (no datetime noise).

HITL: this changes the on-wire format of paginated IDs. Confirm no external client depends on the current format before merging.
acceptance_criteria: DatabaseHelper.fetch_page returns IDs as plain integers; ID encoding happens at the response-shaping layer only; SqidsHelper.encode is pure (same input → same output); GET /tunkin/{periode} responses still contain encoded IDs but in the new deterministic format; a unit test asserts encode/decode round-trip equality
notes: HITL: On-wire ID format will change after removing datetime noise from SqidsHelper.encode. Confirm no external client depends on the current encoded ID format before merging.
close_reason: Changes ready for HITL review. On-wire ID format changed from datetime-noisy to pure Sqids deterministic. Confirm no external client depends on old format before deploying.

title: Extract PermissionChecker so authorization logic is testable without FastAPI
description:
`require_role()` in app/repositories/sys_user.py:199–218 currently decodes a Sqid, queries sys_role_menu, builds a DataFrame, and checks membership — all inside a FastAPI dependency closure. Authorization rules can only be exercised through a full HTTP request.

Extract a pure `PermissionChecker.allows(role_id, menu_codes) → bool` backed by a `MenuLookup` interface. Production MenuLookup hits `sys_role_menu`; tests use an in-memory dict. `require_role()` shrinks to: verify token via TokenVerifier → call PermissionChecker → raise on false. End-to-end: protected endpoints still return 401/403 correctly.
acceptance_criteria: PermissionChecker class exists with no FastAPI imports; MenuLookup interface has at least two adapters (DB-backed and in-memory); require_role() is a thin composition of TokenVerifier + PermissionChecker; unit tests cover PermissionChecker without spinning up FastAPI; existing auth integration tests still pass

title: Split TokenHelper into TokenIssuer and TokenVerifier; break cycle with SysUserRepository
description:
TokenHelper currently instantiates SysUserRepository (circular coupling) and `require_role()` constructs a fresh TokenHelper on every request. Token issuance, token verification, and user lookup are three different concerns tangled into one file.

Split into two pure-function modules: `TokenIssuer` (claims in → JWT out) and `TokenVerifier` (JWT in → claims out, or raise). Neither imports SysUserRepository. The router composes the verifier with the user repo when needed. End-to-end check: `/token`, `/refresh`, `/me`, `/validate` all return the same shape they do today.
acceptance_criteria: TokenIssuer and TokenVerifier are separate classes/modules with no reference to SysUserRepository; SysUserRepository no longer imports JWT code; the four auth endpoints pass an integration test that mints, refreshes, and validates a token end-to-end; TokenVerifier has unit tests that do not import FastAPI

title: Wire repositories and helpers through FastAPI Depends instead of module-level singletons
description:
Module-scoped instantiations (e.g. `repository = TunkinRepository()` in routers/tunkin.py and `token_helper = TokenHelper()` in repositories/tunkin_repository.py) make test injection impossible — every test shares the same instance and `app.dependency_overrides` cannot substitute fakes. Replace these globals with FastAPI `Depends` factories so a clean test seam exists.

End-to-end behavior must be unchanged: `GET /tunkin/{periode}` and `POST /tunkin/upload` and all auth endpoints still work. After this slice, `app.dependency_overrides[TunkinRepository] = ...` substitutes a fake without monkey-patching.
acceptance_criteria: No module-level repository or helper instantiations remain in app/routers/ or app/repositories/; all repositories and helpers obtained via Depends; existing endpoints return identical responses; a smoke test demonstrates app.dependency_overrides successfully swapping a repository

title: Normalize ResponseBuilder usage in exception handlers
description:
The exception handlers in app/main.py instantiate ResponseBuilder() inline on every call (lines 25, 30, 37, 44), which is misleading because ResponseBuilder is effectively stateless — almost every method is @staticmethod or @classmethod, and the only instance methods (from_http_exception, from_exception) don't read self state.

Goal: make the usage consistent — either always go through the existing get_response_builder dependency (used by routers), or always call the static methods directly. Pick one and apply it everywhere in main.py.

Out of scope: redesigning ResponseBuilder, changing routers, or altering the response payload shape.
design:
Two viable styles:
A) Treat ResponseBuilder as a namespace: call ResponseBuilder.unauthorized(...) and ResponseBuilder.from_http_exception_classmethod(exc) statically. This matches what the body already does in most methods.
B) Inject via get_response_builder. This matches how routers already consume it.

Routers already do (B), so (B) gives codebase-wide consistency. But exception handlers in FastAPI can't easily use Depends, so a pragmatic compromise: convert from_http_exception and from_exception to @classmethod and call them as ResponseBuilder.from_http_exception(exc) statically in main.py, removing every inline ResponseBuilder() call.
acceptance_criteria:
1. No more bare ResponseBuilder() instantiation inside exception handlers.
2. All ResponseBuilder calls in main.py use one consistent style (static or classmethod).
3. from_http_exception still works when called from routers (e.g. auth.py uses it via the injected instance) — backward compatibility kept.
4. No behavior change in any response payload or header.

title: Consolidate HTTPException → ResponseBuilder mapping into a single registry
description:
The mapping from HTTP status codes to ResponseBuilder methods is duplicated: `main.py:19–43` (global exception handler) and `response_model.py:379–394` (ResponseBuilder.from_http_exception) both contain if/elif chains. Two copies of the same table — adding a new error class means editing both.

Consolidate into one status-code-to-builder registry inside ResponseBuilder. The global exception handler becomes a one-liner that consults the registry. End-to-end check: raising HTTPException with 400/401/403/404/422/500 each returns the same response shape as today.
acceptance_criteria: A single registry/dispatch table maps status codes to ResponseBuilder methods; main.py exception handler is a thin call into ResponseBuilder; ResponseBuilder.from_http_exception uses the same registry; tests cover all currently-handled status codes and assert identical response payloads before/after
